preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that places a premium on approach play and total strokes gained. The 3M Open offers a strong field and solid betting markets, making it a key mid-summer stop for both outright and placement wagers.
preview.modelExplainer: SG Approach (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) carry the heaviest weights, followed by SG Putting (16.8%) and SG Around the Green (13.3%). Driving accuracy and bogey avoidance receive lighter emphasis, so the model prioritizes players who gain strokes on approach and avoid big numbers.
preview.pickApproach: Top-tier bets focus on players ranked 1-5 in the tournament model who also rank inside the top 25 in power rank. Mid-tier wagers target 6-15 while monitoring SG approach and putting edges. Lower-tier exposure is limited to 16-20 only when course-specific stats align strongly.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Model ranks Jake Knapp first and Scottie Scheffler second entering the 144-player field at TPC Twin Cities.
article.introduction: The tournament model places Jake Knapp at tournament rank 1 with 2.002 SG total and Scottie Scheffler at tournament rank 2 with 1.868 SG total. Course weights emphasize SG approach at 25.8 percent and SG total at 22.8 percent. Post-Open context shows varied workload and FedExCup positioning among the top model rows.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: TPC Twin Cities hosts the 3M Open with SG approach weighted at 25.8 percent and SG total at 22.8 percent. The model ranks Jake Knapp first overall and Scottie Scheffler second overall. No outright, top-10, or top-20 targets were generated this week.

sections.heading: Course Factors
sections.body: SG approach carries the highest weight at 25.8 percent while SG around-the-green sits at 13.3 percent. SG putting is weighted at 16.8 percent and SG off-the-tee at 11.4 percent. Driving accuracy holds a 3.9 percent weight and bogey avoidance a 3.0 percent weight.

sections.heading: Weekly Betting Angles
sections.body: Jake Knapp leads the model at tournament rank 1 with 2.002 SG total and 1.285 SG putting. Scottie Scheffler sits at tournament rank 2 with 1.868 SG total and 0.623 SG off-the-tee. Sudarshan Yellamaraju ranks third at tournament rank 3 with 1.34 SG total.

sections.heading: Post-Open Workload and Motivation
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler posted a top-5 Open finish and enters with six rounds over two weeks at FedExCup rank 1. Jake Knapp missed the cut at The Open and logged four rounds over two weeks while sitting at FedExCup rank 39. Kurt Kitayama recorded eight rounds over two weeks and holds FedExCup rank 32.

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: No outright targets were generated this week per the provided betting card.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: No top-10 targets were generated this week per the provided betting card.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: No top-20 targets were generated this week per the provided betting card.

sections.heading: Players to Approach Cautiously
sections.body: Austin Smotherman ranks seventh in the model with 0.874 SG approach yet sits at power rank 37 and holds FedExCup rank 78. Sam Stevens ranks ninth with 0.545 SG off-the-tee but carries power rank 45 and FedExCup rank 53.

article.conclusion: Model tier: Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler. Secondary tier: Sudarshan Yellamaraju and Hideki Matsuyama. No outright, top-10, or top-20 cards generated.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.
